conclusion: At the pre-registered cutoffs (monomer lDDT>=0.7, complex DockQ>=0.23) the leakage-controlled 21-target set is 100%/100% correct -> Boltz-2 succeeds on recent novel-fold structures too -> NO routing headroom. Stakes appear only for monomers at lDDT>=0.9 (5/16), the post-hoc cutoff v1 was criticized for. The low-stakes problem is INTRINSIC to recent high-resolution structures, independent of leakage. Running the >=3-model x >=3-seed LLM routing pilot on a 100%-base-rate substrate would repeat v1's error (trust-all already optimal); by the project's own NO-GO discipline the routing pilot is not run here.
